url.markdownDescription:
Host HTTP/HTTPS address to connect and write to

Specifies an HTTP/HTTPS address to transmit TenXObject instance/template field values

headers.markdownDescription:
Request HTTP header pairs

Specifies a list of HTTP header values to accompany requests in the form of <name>=<value> (e.g., header1=hello)

method.markdownDescription:
HTTP request method

HTTP method to use (e.g., GET, POST, DELETE). (Default: POST)

fields.markdownDescription:
List of TenXObject field names to encode

Specifies a list of TenXObject intrinsic/calculated/extracted field names to extract and write to the output.

filter.markdownDescription:
A JavaScript expression an TenXObject must evaluate as 'truthy' against to be written to this output

Specifies a JavaScript expression that an TenXObject must evaluate as truthy to write its field/template field values to this output. For example, 'outputHttpFilter: this.timestamp' will only emit [timestamped](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+timestamped) TenXObjects.

writeObjects.markdownDescription:
If true, encode TenXObjects [outputHttpFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/http/#outputhttpfields) to this output

Determine whether to write [outputHttpFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/http/#outputhttpfields) values of an TenXObject passed to this output. This configuration is typically defined using a JavaScript expression.  To enable/disable the output depending on whether a launch argument, environment variable, or JVM -D option evaluates as truthy, use: ``` yaml   outputHttpWriteObjects: $=TenXEnv.get("myHttpAddress") ``` To learn more see [TenXEnv.get](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXEnv.get). (Default: true)

writeTemplates.markdownDescription:
If true, write new TenXTemplates to this output

Controls whether to write template values of TenXObjects passed to this output. The output will only emit TenXTemplates that the pipeline did not load at startup via the [templateFile](https://doc.log10x.com/run/template/#templatefiles) argument, and will only emit an TenXTemplate once based on its[templateHash](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+templateHash) value. (Default: false)

encodeType.markdownDescription:
The output format in which to encode TenXObject values. Possible values:[json, delimited]

Specifies how to encode [outputHttpFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/http/#outputhttpfields) values to this output. Possible values: - **json**: formats names and values for the current TenXObject as: {"field":"str","field2":1, "field3": true} - **delimited**: formats values for the current TenXObject as: "str",1,true    The [outputHttpEncodeDelimiter](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/http/#outputhttpencodedelimiter) argument determines the separator to use. (Default: delimited)

encodedLinePrefix.markdownDescription:
The line prefix prepended to the encoded event if 'outputHttpEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the char which is prepended to the emitted TenXObject field values when [outputHttpEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/http/#outputhttpencodetype) is `delimited`. (Default: )

encodeDelimiter.markdownDescription:
The delimiter placed between key and values pairs if 'outputHttpEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the char delimiter to separate TenXObject field values when [outputHttpEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/http/#outputhttpencodetype) is `delimited`. (Default:  )
